qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded batch operations cause resource exhaustion and denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets POST /batch processing in src/batch.js where Promise.all handles an unbounded requests array; the advisory targets missing REST/GraphQL query complexity limits for nested subqueries, includes, and GraphQL field traversal.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: LDAP injection in groupFilter permits authentication bypass
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets unsanitized LDAP groupFilter substitution in src/Adapters/Auth/LDAP.js; the advisory targets request-complexity enforcement gaps in Parse Server REST and GraphQL query handling.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is Parse Server denial of service from unbounded REST and GraphQL query complexity, fixed by requestComplexity limits for subquery depth, include depth/count, and GraphQL depth/field count. The qualifying findings instead cover unbounded batch operations and LDAP filter injection.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-cmj3-wx7h-ffvg describes unauthenticated resource exhaustion through crafted REST/GraphQL queries and missing requestComplexity limits.
batch_operation_resource_exhaustion reports POST /batch unbounded operation fan-out in src/batch.js:67-146, a different endpoint and root cause from query complexity limits.
ldap_injection_auth_bypass reports LDAP filter injection in src/Adapters/Auth/LDAP.js:143, a different component and security failure mode from query complexity limits.
